nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving rivers and streams of the Indian subcontinent, where it grows submersed over silty and sandy substrates in soft, gentle current.

decorPreferences: open background space for tall strap-like leaves

summary: An Indian-subcontinent crypt with long, narrow, strap-like leaves that resembles its close relative Cryptocoryne spiralis. A heavy root feeder reaching 40-50 cm, well suited to background placement in medium-to-large planted aquariums.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne retrospiralis is a tall, grass-like Cryptocoryne native to slow rivers and streams of India and Bangladesh, very similar in habit to the better-known Cryptocoryne spiralis (fw-plant-065). It produces long, narrow, ribbon-like leaves that arch out from a basal rosette, making it a natural background plant in 60+ cm aquariums; mature specimens commonly reach 40-50 cm, with Buce Plant citing up to 24 in (~60 cm) and Modern Aquarium reporting ~20-38 cm depending on strain and conditions. It is a heavy root feeder and does best planted into a nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs; low-to-moderate light is sufficient, and CO2 injection is beneficial but not required. Both sources note that, like other crypts, it can melt after shipping or sudden parameter changes — trim affected leaves and leave the rhizome undisturbed, and the plant typically regrows from the crown within a couple of weeks. Temperature consensus is roughly 21-28°C (Modern Aquarium 70-82°F). pH consensus is 6.5-7.5; hardness was not reported by either primary source and was inferred from the close congener C. spiralis (2-12 dGH/dKH) — see needs_review status if more conservative bounds are required. Care level is widely described as easy once established; major caution is against relocating an already-rooted specimen.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary source. Confirmed Indian/Bangladeshi origin, 70-82°F (~21-28°C) temperature, ~20-38 cm height, low-to-moderate light, optional CO2, root-feeder substrate with root tabs, slow-to-moderate growth, midground-to-background placement, easy-to-moderate care level, and crypt-melt risk on shipping/parameter swings.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Secondary source. Confirmed pH 6.5-7.5, 10-24 in (~25-60 cm) height, moderate light, CO2 optional but beneficial, medium growth rate, mid/background placement, runner propagation, easy care, family Araceae, and sensitivity to relocation/strong light. Lists origin as India, China, Thailand — Indian origin matches Modern Aquarium and Wikipedia; the China/Thailand attribution is broader than other sources support and was not adopted.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Cryptocoryne retrospiralis)
additional.notes: Attempted as tertiary corroboration for taxonomy and native range; URL returned HTTP 404 on both attempts and could not be used. Family Araceae / order Alismatales taken from genus-level knowledge and the C. spiralis sibling entry rather than from this source.
